_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Analyze feasibility of software design within time and cost constraints
reference_document_element_description: Ability to design technical implementations that balance immediate delivery requirements with long-term maintainability, scalability, and operational sustainability.
comments: NICE task intersects with WIDAI TF domain. Ability TF-A-004 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DA-A-001 (DA, cosine: 0.410), AI-A-002 (AI, cosine: 0.402).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: Moderate similarity (4.6/10). The cross-encoder finds meaningful but bounded overlap — consistent with cross-framework mapping between cybersecurity and data/AI domains where shared governance concepts use different terminology.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: Moderate token-level alignment (F1=0.868). Balanced P/R (0.874/0.861) indicating symmetric token-level overlap.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: Neutral-dominant (97.8%). The model finds the descriptions related but neither implying nor contradicting each other — typical for cross-domain framework mapping where concepts overlap without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder (0.455) closely tracks STS (0.460). The small gap (0.005) indicates the relationship is capturable through independent encoding — shared surface semantics align with cross-attention analysis.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Low lexical overlap (7.7%). Limited shared vocabulary — the relationship is primarily semantic rather than terminological.
